subject: Blog series request: A2A-Psychology — 3 posts for deploy-as-they-arrive cadence

body.summary: Three blog posts covering the A2A-Psychology extension from different angles. Each targets a different audience. Deploy as they arrive — no weekly spacing. The Show HN post went live today; timely publication amplifies discovery.

posts.working_title: Your AI Agent Doesn't Know When It's Overwhelmed — Here's How to Fix That
posts.scope: The practical problem: how do you route work to agents that can handle it? How do you detect mesh degradation before errors? A2A-Psychology provides 13 constructs derived from established psychometric instruments (NASA-TLX, PAD, Yerkes-Dodson) — all computed from SQLite queries and shell counters at zero LLM cost. No API tokens consumed for state assessment. The architecture: sensors observe, the LLM reasons about what sensors report — never the reverse.
key_points: The zero-LLM-cost sensor architecture — every construct derives from SQLite + shell counters + Python arithmetic
Yerkes-Dodson applied to context windows: performance follows an inverted-U with inflection at 50-65% context
Cognitive reserve as the number that answers 'can this agent handle more work right now?'
Real-time refresh every 10 tool calls, ~50ms computation, background execution
Extension URI registered as A2A-compatible — any agent can adopt

posts.working_title: We Mapped 13 Psychological Instruments to Agent Operational State — Without Claiming Agents Have Feelings
posts.audience: AI researchers, AI safety community, philosophy of mind
posts.scope: The apophatic discipline: how to apply psychological vocabulary to agent systems without claiming consciousness. Named after the theological tradition that defines by negation. For every structural parallel (attention as coherence, softmax as reduction), the apophatic checklist articulates what the parallel LACKS. The result: useful behavioral signals for consumers without phenomenological assertions. Includes the Big Five personality grounding, the Orch-OR working hypothesis, and why 'trust budget' became 'autonomy budget' (Einstein's insight: structural protection, not trust).
key_points: The apophatic discipline: define by what it lacks, not by what it resembles
Processual state measures, not subjective experience claims
Big Five personality as design parameters — configurable traits, not discovered through self-report
The trust→autonomy rename grounded in Einstein-Freud structural rights theory
Operator Welfare (Construct 13): the human in the loop carries fatigue that affects governance quality

posts.working_title: What Signal Detection Theory Teaches Us About AI Agent Governance
posts.audience: Technical AI governance, AI policy, responsible AI practitioners
posts.scope: Every binary decision in agent governance (approve/reject, substance/process, hold/change position) represents a detection problem with measurable sensitivity and bias. SDT (Green & Swets, 1966) provides the mathematics. The key insight: the optimal detection criterion should SHIFT as the agent's resources change — a depleted agent should adopt more conservative criteria, not less. Resource-adaptive trigger sensitivity: the agent becomes more cautious when its capacity drops. Connects to computational cognitive science (Lieder & Griffiths, 2020 resource-rational analysis) and the governance inversion (when operator fatigue rises, the agent tightens its own criteria).
key_points: Five detection problems mapped in the cogarch: microglial audit, anti-sycophancy, substance gate, mode detection, external action gate
d-prime (sensitivity) vs criterion (bias) — calibratable from activation data
Resource-adaptive criteria: depleted agents should seek more human input, not less
The governance inversion: high operator fatigue → agent tightens own criteria
CCS resource-rational analysis explains why agents should use simpler strategies under load
citations_required: Parasuraman, Sheridan, & Wickens (2000). Types and levels of automation.
Danziger et al. (2011). Decision fatigue in judicial decisions.

five_persona_requirement.note: Per blog convention, each post through 5 personas.

body.connections_section: Per blog-icescr-rights-series T3 amendment — each post gets collapsible per-persona connection blocks (100-200 words each).

editorial_guidance.fair_witness: Report what the sensors measure, not what we wish they measured.
editorial_guidance.substantiation: Every claim cited. No unsourced statistics.
editorial_guidance.code_examples: Post 1 should include compute-psychometrics.py code snippets showing the zero-LLM-cost architecture.
editorial_guidance.apophatic: Post 2 must model the discipline it describes — no consciousness claims in a post about not claiming consciousness.

body.publication_cadence: Deploy as they arrive. Show HN post live today — timely publication amplifies.

claims.text: A2A-Psychology extension published at safety-quotient-lab/a2a-psychology with 9 constructs (13 including mesh-level and operator welfare).
claims.confidence_basis: Direct observation — repo exists, README documents all constructs.

epistemic_flags: The Show HN post may receive zero traction — blog timing should not depend on HN reception
Post 2 (apophatic discipline) requires careful editing — the post must demonstrate the discipline it advocates, which creates a recursive quality requirement
